item.title: This Founding Father Died in Disgrace. But He Can’t Be Forgotten.
item.description: Excluded from America’s origin story, James Wilson may have been the most influential, prescient, and democratic drafter of the Constitution.
item.dc:creator: Jesse Wegman

item.title: The Wall the Tohono O’odham Don’t Want
item.description: Trump threatens native sovereignty in Arizona.
item.dc:creator: Geraldo L. Cadava

item.title: Trump’s Second Gamble on Iran
item.description: If the regime can’t be dislodged by force, maybe it can be bribed.
item.dc:creator: Karim Sadjadpour

item.title: Britain’s Next Leader Has Emerged
item.description: Andy Burnham, Manchester’s mayor, prepares to challenge Keir Starmer—and is likely to win.
item.dc:creator: Helen Lewis

item.title: The Least Logical Fashion Rule
item.description: Shorts at the office make great sense.
item.dc:creator: Gilad Edelman

item.title: The Left-Wing Case Against Anti-Zionism
item.description: Discrimination against Israelis qua Israelis—and the “Zionists” who appear as their proxies—is a moral wrong.
item.dc:creator: Adam Louis-Klein

item.title: If Only Trump Knew What Vance Is Doing
item.description: If the president is infallible, there must be some other explanation for his Iran defeat.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Chait

item.title: Iran Has Humiliated Trump
item.description: Officials in Tehran got the United States to sign a document that even Americans described as degrading, mortifying, a total capitulation.
item.dc:creator: Graeme Wood

item.title: What Did You Expect?
item.description: Perhaps this was always how Trump’s ill-conceived war was destined to end.
item.dc:creator: Daniel B. Shapiro

item.title: All the Sad Hawks
item.description: Neoconservatives are struggling to reconcile their hopes for Trump with the failure of his Iran war.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Chait
